concept.title: Technical Service Manual

section.title: Summary
section.p: Information Bulletin 1065 will be released on a regular basis to provide updated Colorado service information on multiple topics.

section.title: 1 New chiller due to legislation requirements
section.p: New USA and Canada legislation requires chillers with updated refrigerants.
The previous chiller (PN:1070119965) used R134a;
the new model (PN:4120068863) uses R513a.
Cut in SN in the factory: 990402698

section.title: 2 IB1094: Security Bulletin October 2025
section.p: Security update Colorado C1630/C1650/CMxx status October 2025.
See description if your product can be updated.

section.title: 3 PRISMAservice R2.2, ADAM R2.1 and HTML TSM R2.6 release
section.p: More information on these releases can be found in the service documentation section of PRISMAservice, see below.

section.title: 4 Safety When Working with UV(gel) Inks: Key Reminders
section.p: In today's fast-paced world, where everyone is busy and tasks need to be completed quickly, it's only human to sometimes overlook important safety guidelines. However, taking a moment to remember and follow these rules can make all the difference, especially when working with potentially hazardous materials like UV(gel) inks.

When handling UV(gel) inks, always use preventive measures. These inks contain reactive chemicals that can irritate or damage the skin, and repeated exposure may lead to allergic reactions or even chemical burns. Typical exposure scenarios include handling cartridges, cleaning spills, or touching contaminated surfaces, so proper Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is essential.

Always wear chemical-resistant nitrile gloves (at least 0.4 mm thick), ensure gloves overlap sleeves, and use safety glasses and long-sleeved clothing. Avoid latex or vinyl gloves, as they offer poor protection. Dispose of contaminated gloves as chemical waste.

If ink contacts your skin, wipe it off immediately with dry cloth, then wash thoroughly with soap and water and never use solvents or alcohol. Report any recurring irritation to your supervisor or medical staff and follow local hazardous waste rules for disposal.
Remember: Uncured UV(gel) ink is hazardous. Consistent use of PPE and good hygiene are your best defense, even for experienced users. Safety always comes first.

section.title: 5 Cut in date and SN of MOD50 in factory
section.p: The pump rotors with four rollers will be introduced in the factory from 3rd of November 2025, Cut-in Serial Number: 990402731.

section.title: 6 Cut in date and availability of power supply with conformal coating.
section.p: A new power supply with conformal coating, PN: 4120066746, POWER SUPPLY 48V 1600W RSP-1600-48, will be implemented on October 30, 2025.
The initial cut-in serial number will be 990402730.
Future orders for service parts will also include conformal coating.
This update is part of the ongoing development of the Colorado platform.

section.title: 1 Measurement & Control scanner performance tile
section.p: In the advanced technical views, a new tile is available: the Measurement & Control scanner performance.
This tile can be used to check if the scanner needs cleaning. Values above 90% scanner brightness are considered clean.
The white background of the scanner is recalibrated and measured after every following event:
• PH-calibration
• Carriage calibration
• Nozzleoptimization
• SDS21-6-004: Measure print characteristics (Y-errors)
In case the same media is used, then a big change in scanner brightness might indicate a polluted scanner by e.g. ink aerosol.

section.title: 2 Attention White Printhead, incorrectly replacement
section.p: Attention asked for incorrectly replaced white printheads.
Printhead score for white printheads can be very misleading.
Print mode is an important factor.
When white is not used, drying in of the white ink can occur resulting in deviating/failing nozzles and therefore a lower printhead score.
A purge/wipe recovers the nozzle performance as can be seen in a white print mode.
Always check the white printhead score over a longer time window and also when white is used.
Job settings can be seen in the job settings of the Engine Cycle ID overview Tile of PRISMA Service.

section.title: 3 Advice on MRE: Machine Recoverable Error
section.p: In some case we observed that parts have been replaced only after 1 MRE (Machine Recoverable Error).
A MRE might be resolved by a reboot.
CPP's recommendation is always to perform a reboot to see whether the error has been resolved.
This preferable can be done by the customer himself and asked by the complaint desk.
This can prevent unnecessary replacement of parts and visits.
Next to that the printer might be again up and running.

section.title: 4 Update of the functional description of v2 chiller
section.p: The functional description of the v2 chiller has been updated:

li.p: Now it is clear what belongs to the chiller top-assembly
Also the different cooling/heating flow are now easy recognizable

section.title: 5 Technical view: changes since last visit in 03 extended
section.p: The technical view "02 System info extended" contains a 'Changes' tab with the parameters/counters changes since last visit.

section.title: 6 New Technical view: History of parameters/counters
section.p: New technical views have been added:
Both can show the history between datalog snapshots (visits + current snapshot).
Parameter view includes an indication if something has changed between 2 or more snapshots counters include a trend estimation based on snapshot data.

section.title: About this guide
section.p: This Technical Service Manual contains information, which helps an educated service technician to maintain, to repair, and to keep the system up and running.

section.title: Important TSM updates
section.p: See Important TSM updates

section.title: Safety use
section.p: Always observe the safety information.
See Instructions for safe use (Technician)

section.title: Copyright and trademarks
section.p: Which copyright and trademark information is used in this manual?
See Copyright and Trademarks

section.p: See Notes for the reader

section.p: See Available documentation
